sys_metadata_link.payload:
true text 89137fd0db4420106a58ef92ca961903 false false false false true 6a0e0e96db4d99501e1792c5d4961956 ddba8256db4d99501e1792c5d496195a Important: Make changes to Tasker groups after regular business hours. This is important because changes KB0010015 2022-08-10 4.67 Configuring Uses Groups for Roles in Tasker kb_knowledge rjoy 2022-08-10 02:59:37 global / d5747ad0dbd111901e1792c5d496197f 36 csloyer 2022-09-22 14:55:59 470 Important: Make changes to Tasker groups after regular business hours. This is important because changes to groups impact ServiceNow and Tasker system settings. Making updates after hours ensures that all users receive applicable updates.

Tasker Groups Management leverages standard ServiceNow users and groups functionality to enable a group administrator (Tasker Application Administrator or Platform Administrator) to assign users to Tasker groups and manage groups over time.

When enabling Groups Management, you must must select a group for each Tasker role and select the Uses Groups for Roles checkbox. If the checkbox is not selected, you must assign Tasker roles to individual Tasker member records.

Overview:

  1. Create groups for each Tasker role.
  2. Select a group for each Tasker role and assign the Tasker role to each group.
  3. Select the Uses Groups for Roles checkbox.
  4. Save changes.
  5. Add users to groups to assign them the Tasker role for the group.

Refer to the following for help with using the Groups Management page and a reference of Tasker roles enabled for Groups Management.

Groups Management Icons, Menus, and Messages

  • Sync. Use the sync function to synchronize records between the ServiceNow Role Members and Tasker Member Table Roles tables. (For example, after removing a user from a group, the Tasker role is removed from the Role Members table. Select Sync to remove the role from Member Table Rows.)
    • Selecting the Sync button at the top of the screen synchronizes all changes made to Tasker roles.

    • As shown below, you can synchronize specific Tasker roles by selecting Sync Group from ellipses menus and from ”Let’s fix it” messages.
  • Ellipses menus (…). Select an ellipsis to the right of a Tasker role to open a pop-up menu with options to: Edit Group, Add Group, and Sync Group.

  • “i” icon to the right of Tasker roles. Select the icon to open the Counts and Knowledge Base Article tabs for the selected role.

    • Counts tab (for each role). Includes: Group Members, Has Role Explicitly, Role Members, and Member Table Roles lists.
    • Knowledge Base Article tab (for each role). Select the tab for help with the selected role. Links to Related Articles (for the Counts tab) are at the bottom of each article.
  • Buttons:
    • Save Changes. Select Save Changes to save group selections for Tasker roles. You must select a group for every Tasker role.
    • Cancel. Selecting Cancel removes unsaved updates to group selections. Closing or refreshing the browser will also remove unsaved changes to group selections.
  • Informational Icons and Messages:
    • Triangle icon (warning). See below for examples.
    • Circle icon (required action). See below for examples.
    • “This group is missing the role.” This message displays when you select a group for a Tasker role and the Tasker role is not assigned to the group. Selecting “Add the role now” automatically assigns the Tasker role to the group.

 

    • “Users explictily assigned to role.” This message displays when users are explictily assigned a Tasker role, but do not belong to the group. This is common when upgrading from an earlier version of Tasker (2.7.x and earlier). Selecting “Let’s fix it” adds users, who have been assigned a Tasker role on their user record, to the group.

    • “A group is not selected for the role. Select a group to resolve.” This message indicates that you have not selected a Tasker group for the role. You must select a group for each Tasker role.

 

    • Sync the ‘Role Members’ with the ‘Member Table Role.’ This message indicates mismatches between the ServiceNow Role Members and Tasker Member Table Role tables. Select “Let’s fix it” to synchronize these tables. However, this message does not necessarily indicate an issue, as users may be assigned to a role through group permissions, but not assigned to a Tasker Organizational Unit (OU). Synchronizing these tables may take time and should be done sparingly.

 

Tasker Roles Reference Table

Tasker groups are available for the organization-level roles listed below.

Tasker Roles Role Name

What It Does

Roles Required for Group

Tasker Application Administrator

Allows users to manage all application tables and assign permissions to users.

x_153465_discovert.tasker.application.admin

Global Hierarchy Administrator

Allows users to manage members and member permissions of all Organizational Units (OU).

x_153465_discovert.tasker_organizational_admin

Task Administrator

Allows users to manage tasks and task assignment for all Organizational Units and members.

x_153465_discovert.tasker_global_Tasking_Authority_member_admin

Task Executive

Allows users to manage tasks and task assignment for all Organizational Units and the members. Unlike Task Administrators, Task Executives only receive banner notifications for assigned tasks and tasks they assign to others.

x_153465_discovert.Task_Executive

Template Designer Permissions Administrator

Allows users to grant template designer permissions to other users.

x_153465_discovert.tasker_process_template_admin

 

Global Template Designer

Allows users to design global task templates. (Templates for creating organization-level templates).

x_153465_discovert.tasker_process_template_global

Personal Template Designer

Allows users to create task templates for personal use.

x_153465_discovert.tasker_process_template_global

Tasker User

Enables users to use the Tasker application. With Tasker User permissions, users can receive and complete tasks, task others, and use the My Tasks dashboard).

x_153465_discovert.tasker_user

 

In an upcoming Tasker release, OU roles, such as Task Manager and Organizational Unit Hierarchy Administrator and restricted groups will be available for group management.

Tip: To assign an OU role, such as Task Manager, a user with Organizational Hierarchy permissions (such as Global Hierarchy Administrator or Organizational Unit Hierarchy Administrator) must add the user to a Tasker OU and select the Tasker role on the member record.
